The '''''A''-class bulk freighter''' was a type of container [[Starship/Legends|ship]] that gained popularity among [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]] expeditions shortly before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It could also be referred to as an '''A-class bulk freighter'''.

In later years, ships of this type were found in a variety of roles. During the [[Battle of Sluis Van]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] sneaked a force of cloaked [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] into the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] inside the hull of a PCL 27 by the name of ''[[Nartissteu]]''. Another ship of this class was the ''[[Black Lotus]]'', converted by the [[Camarina Connection]] to serve as a mobile drug laboratory.

==Behind the scenes==
The "PCL 27" originates in an entry on the ''Black Lotus'' in the [[Casus Belli 89|September/October 1995]] issue of the French RPG magazine [[Casus Belli (magazine)|''Casus Belli'']], supplied with an illustration by [[Fred Blanchard]], illustrator on the [[Star Wars: Heir to the Empire (comic series)|comic adaptation]] of ''[[Heir to the Empire]]''. This magazine predates by several months the appearance of a ship of this type in [[Heir to the Empire 6|issue 6]] of the comic in March [[1996]], but it appears that the background material has never been translated into English and thus is considered non-canon as it was never approved by [[Lucas Licensing]].
